mer was seriously injured and taken
to the Sacred Heart hospital, where
this noon he is reported as very low.


The story of the wreck as told by
one of the belated passengers is as
follows:


"A freight train struck a rock slide
near Wenatchce while going about 80
miles an hour. 1t was on down
grade and the engine was turned over
on its side and several freight cars
piled up in n heap.


THE engineer was taken from the
wreck and sent to Spokane on a spe-
cial. The injured conductor and
brakeman remained at the scene and
assisted in clearing the wreck until
they were exhausted"


"A SECOND WRECK.


Newly arrived passengers say that
the Great Northern train which left
Spokane Monday evening at 8 o'clock
was wrecked at n small station
